com.mentor.chs.plugin.constraint

Interface IXCavityDetailConstraintResult


  • @NotToBeImplemented
public interface IXCavityDetailConstraintResult
    Result object that contains a set of wire combinations, each combination is a set of wires. The object provides a way to tell if the generator would fail to generate combinations through setReasonForFailure

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      boolean addCombination(java.util.Set<? extends IXWire> combination)
      Adds a set of wires (single combination) to the collection of combinations.
      java.util.Set<? extends java.util.Set<? extends IXWire>> getCombinations()
      Returns the set of combinations populated for the client.
      java.lang.String getReasonForFailure()
      Returns the string containing the textual representation of the reason for failure.
      void setReasonForFailure(java.lang.String reason)
      If the generator is supposed to fail, set the 'reason' string.

    • Method Detail

      • addCombination

        boolean addCombination(java.util.Set<? extends IXWire> combination)
        Adds a set of wires (single combination) to the collection of combinations. The tool would then create a cavity detail object for each combination.
        Parameters:
        combination - - Set of wires that represent a single combination
        Returns:
        'true' if the added combination is valid (e.g. all wires are connected to the cavity), or 'false' otherwise.

      • setReasonForFailure

        void setReasonForFailure(java.lang.String reason)
        If the generator is supposed to fail, set the 'reason' string. This string will be shown in the log relevant to the cavity component management action the user is running.
        Parameters:
        reason - text message to log/display if the constraint failed. The reason is null by default. Set it to a non-null value to instruct the generator to fail.

      • getCombinations

        java.util.Set<? extends java.util.Set<? extends IXWire>> getCombinations()
        Returns the set of combinations populated for the client. Used by the tool to create a cavity detail object per combination.
        Returns:
        Returns a set of wire combinations. Each combination is another set of wires. Returns an empty set if no combinations got generated.

      • getReasonForFailure

        java.lang.String getReasonForFailure()
        Returns the string containing the textual representation of the reason for failure. Failure reason is set by setReasonForFailure
        Returns:
        Returns the text message set by setReasonForFailure method, or 'null' by default if no reason was set.
